channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Aura’s delightful Aspen photo frame is on sale for $30 off this weekend
item.description: Aura’s digital frames are kind of like living photo albums that get better with time, which is why we often recommend them for Mother’s Day. They’re gifts that keep on giving, in a way, and right...

item.title: How Spring Security Works Internally (Filters, Authentication & Authorization Explained)
item.description: If you have worked with Spring Boot for a while, you have used Spring Security without fully tracing what happens inside it. You add a dependency, configure a SecurityFilterChain, and wire a...

item.title: Asyncio - in Python Explained Like You’re Ordering Food (A Beginner Guide)
item.description: If you’ve ever written Python code that feels slow when doing things like API calls or waiting for data... you’ve already felt the problem that asyncio solves. Async basically means “Don’t wait -...

item.title: What is Meta-Architect?
item.description: When we talk about AI coding agents, most tools are great at “fill in this function” and pretty weak at “is this system actually well-designed?”. Meta-Architect is live on GitHub. Repo link:...

item.title: The Story of Building Zoom's Video Compression 2026 – C++ and WebAssembly 2.0
item.description: The Story of Building Zoom's 2026 Video Compression: C++ and WebAssembly 2.0 In early 2024, Zoom’s engineering team faced a critical inflection point: the video conferencing platform’s decade-old...

item.title: The Heartbeat Frame
item.description: Long time no see… Again! I just survived my exams a day ago and I could not help but jump straight to writing this blog! Since this blog does not have something I have done already, this is a...

item.title: What I Shipped in April Building Invoiso(v4.0.5)
item.description: April was one of the busiest months so far while building Invoiso, a free offline invoice and billing tool for Windows and Linux. This month was less about one big headline feature and more about...

item.title: From Vibe Coding to Clarity: Auditing My Own Backend Project
item.description: I built a student internship management system in 3 days, despite an injured hand. It worked. It got me through my defense. But here's the truth: I didn't fully understand every line I wrote. So...

item.title: The Performance Battle benchmark SolidJS deep dive React Server Components: A Practical Guide
item.description: After benchmarking 14 production-grade frontends over 6 months, SolidJS outperformed React Server Components (RSC) by 42% in first-contentful-paint and 58% in time-to-interactive for client-heavy...

item.title: Canonical under DDoS: what my Railway logs and uptime say about my real exposure
item.description: Canonical under DDoS: what my Railway logs and uptime say about my real exposure Why do we assume shared infrastructure "just works" until it stops working for someone else? I spent years living...

item.title: Canonical bajo DDoS: lo que mis logs de Railway y uptime dicen sobre mi exposición real
item.description: Canonical bajo DDoS: lo que mis logs de Railway y uptime dicen sobre mi exposición real ¿Por qué asumimos que la infraestructura compartida "simplemente funciona" hasta que deja de funcionar para...

item.title: The best AI dictation apps, tested and ranked
item.description: AI-powered dictation apps are useful for replying to emails, taking notes, and even coding through your voice

item.title: Anker’s discounted 2-in-1 USB-C cable is a great way to spend $15
item.description: I’ll never stop gushing about 2-in-1 USB-C cables. They’re really nice to have because, at this point, I’ve amassed so many devices that charge via USB-C. It’s also common for more than one to...

item.title: Playing Esoteric Ebb is like rolling the dice with a great DM
item.description: It took me a while to get into Esoteric Ebb, a new CRPG from developer Christoffer Bodegård. The elevator pitch is basically Disco Elysium, but in the fantasy style of Dungeons & Dragons: You play...

item.title: The things we’re building
item.description: Hi, friends! Welcome to Installer No. 126, your guide to the best and Verge-iest stuff in the world. (If you're new here, welcome, I need 10 or 15 skirts from Calvin Klein, and also you can read...

item.title: Disneyland Now Uses Face Recognition on Visitors
item.description: Plus: The NSA tests Anthropic’s Mythos Preview to find vulnerabilities, a Finnish teen is charged over the Scattered Spider hacking spree, and more.

item.title: Trellix Confirms Source Code Breach With Unauthorized Repository Access
item.description: Cybersecurity company Trellix has announced that it suffered a breach that enabled unauthorized access to a "portion" of its source code. It said it "recently identified" the compromise of its...

item.title: Replit’s Amjad Masad on the Cursor deal, fighting Apple, and why he’d rather not sell
item.description: At TechCrunch's sold-out StrictlyVC event in San Francisco on Thursday night, we covered a lot of ground in a short time, beginning with the question everyone in the industry is asking right now:...

item.title: Splatoon Raiders preorders for the Switch 2 are nearly 20 percent off
item.description: Nintendo recently announced a new pricing policy, which knocks $10 off the cost of digital versions of future first-party titles exclusive to the Nintendo Switch 2. Splatoon Raiders, for instance,...

item.title: Meta buys robotics startup to bolster its humanoid AI ambitions
item.description: Meta bought humanoid startup Assured Robot Intelligence to beef up its AI models for robots, the company said.

item.title: Musk v. Altman week 1: Elon Musk says he was duped, warns AI could kill us all, and admits that xAI distills OpenAI’s models
item.description: In the first week of the landmark trial between Elon Musk and OpenAI, Musk took the stand in a crisp black suit and tie and argued that OpenAI CEO Sam Altman and president Greg Brockman had...

item.title: Microsoft tests redesigned Windows 11 Run menu with dark mode and more
item.description: Microsoft is testing its much-needed refresh for the Windows 11 Run menu, offering a modern interface that the company says is faster and comes with support for dark mode. The redesign is rolling...

item.title: Apple raises the Mac Mini’s starting price
item.description: Apple's Mac Mini now starts at $799 after the company pulled the $599 option with 256GB of storage from its online store, as spotted earlier by MacRumors. The model's discontinuation comes just...

item.title: A Dark-Money Campaign Is Paying Influencers to Frame Chinese AI as a Threat
item.description: Build American AI, a nonprofit linked to a super PAC bankrolled by executives at OpenAI and Andreessen Horowitz, is funding a campaign to spread pro-AI messaging and stoke fears about China.

item.title: All the evidence revealed so far in Musk v. Altman
item.description: The Musk v. Altman trial is underway, and that means exhibits, or the evidence to be presented in court, are being revealed piece by piece. So far, email exchanges, photos, and corporate documents...

item.title: Ubuntu infrastructure has been down for more than a day
item.description: The outage has hampered communication concerning a critical vulnerability that gives root.

item.title: Birdfy’s smart bird feeder is down to its best-ever price for Mother’s Day
item.description: From crafting machines to smart calendars, a number of unique gifts are already on sale in the run-up to Mother’s Day, May 10th. Birdfy smart feeders are another discounted option to consider,...

item.title: 30,000 Facebook Accounts Hacked via Google AppSheet Phishing Campaign
item.description: A newly discovered Vietnamese-linked operation has been observed using a Google AppSheet as a "phishing relay" to distribute phishing emails with an aim to compromise Facebook accounts. The...

item.title: Did you know you can’t steal a charity? Don’t worry. Elon Musk will remind you.
item.description: Elon Musk spent the better part of three days on the witness stand this week in his lawsuit against OpenAI, and it’s already getting messy. Emails, texts, and his own tweets are surfacing in...

item.title: Pentagon inks deals with Nvidia, Microsoft, and AWS to deploy AI on classified networks
item.description: The deals come as the DOD has doubled down on diversifying its exposure to AI vendors in the wake of its controversial dispute with Anthropic over usage terms of its AI models.
